arts.ttl: Champions League exit awaits Real Madrid or Man City as new playoffs round reaches dramatic finish
arts.sum: Defending champion Madrid takes a 3-2 lead into the second leg at home in their knockout playoff which Man City coach Pep Guardiola suggested left his team with just a 1% chance to advance. AC Milan was ousted at home by Feyenoord, the fourth-place team in the Dutch league, on Tuesday hours before Atalanta was beaten 5-2 on aggregate score by Club Brugge, second in the Belgian league.

arts.ttl: Atletico Madrid go after Real Madrid again with social media campaign
arts.sum: Atletico Madrid and La Liga President Javier Tebas have very much led the charge when it comes to biting back against the complaints from Real Madrid about officiating in recent weeks. Los Colchoneros in particular have been vocal on social media, and on Tuesday began their own social media campaign. Diego Simeone was criticial of […] The post Atletico Madrid go after Real Madrid again with social media campaign appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola reveals 'lie' he told about blockbuster Real Madrid second leg
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola walks back his comments suggesting the club only have a 1% chance of beating Real Madrid in the Champions League.

arts.ttl: Jude Bellingham red card referee breaks silence as Spanish FA launch investigation
arts.sum: Jude Bellingham was controversially sent off during Real Madrid's 1-1 draw with Osasuna on Saturday and referee Jose Luis Munuera Montero has spoken out about the incident

arts.ttl: Guardiola's trusted surgeon explains rise in ACL injuries
arts.sum: Dr Ramon Cugat, the surgeon who Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola has trusted with treating his players since his Barcelona days, explains the increase in ACL injuries in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola admits he LIED with Manchester City claim - 'You got it right'
arts.sum: Manchester City face Real Madrid on Wednesday evening needing to overturn a one-goal deficit in their Champions League play-off, having suffered a dramatic collapse in the first leg

arts.ttl: Referee removed from duty as connections to clubs including Manchester City and Aston Villa investigated
arts.sum: Fans of the Premier League often wrongly insist that the officiating in the league is much worse than elsewhere in Europe. Indeed, the drama seen in Spanish football on a relatively frequent basis is much more than happens even with media related to the English top flight. Sometimes that drama is motivated by questionable decisions The post Referee removed from duty as connections to clubs including Manchester City and Aston Villa investigated appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid vs Manchester City – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Real Madrid meet Manchester City in the second leg of their Champions League play-off tie tonight. The Spaniards hold a one-goal advantage from the first encounter, following a dramatic late win at the Etihad. Carlo Ancelotti’s side trailed heading into the final four minutes of normal time but goals from Brahim Diaz and Jude Bellingham […] The post Real Madrid vs Manchester City – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid vs Manchester City, 2025 Champions League: Predicted lineups
arts.sum: Real Madrid host Manchester City in the return leg of the Champions League ’s Round of 32. Real Madrid predicted XI: Courtois, Valverde, Rudiger, Asencio, Mendy, Tchouameni, Ceballos, Bellingham, Rodrygo, Vinicius, Mbappe.

arts.ttl: Preview: Can Man City come from behind to beat Real Madrid?
arts.sum: Football writer Ben Bloom analyses the key details as Manchester City travel to Real Madrid for the second leg of their UEFA Champions League playoff tie. For the fourth successive season, either Manchester City or Real Madrid will end the other’s UEFA Champions League campaign when the two sides meet on Wednesday.
